# Claim-to-Evidence Matrix

| Claim | Best proof type | Strongest sources in vault | Caution |
|---|---|---|---|
| Social proof can bias future judgment | Randomized experiment | Muchnik et al. 2013; Salganik et al. 2006 | Effects vary by context and valence. |
| Recommender systems can amplify feedback loops | Simulation/theory + surveys | Chaney et al. 2018; popularity bias survey 2024; feedback loops 2026 | Public platforms use unknown defences. |
| Coordinated accounts can increase outreach | Observational cascade analysis | CIB information spreading 2022; bots 2018 | Coordination is not always deceptive. |
| Fake reviews change purchases and welfare | RCT + structural model | NBER 2023; NBER 2025 | Experimental platforms approximate real marketplaces. |
| Search spam works enough to require enforcement | Official platform intervention | Google March 2024 Search update | Platform claims measure remediation, not every spammer's success. |
| Streaming fraud converts fake attention into money | Legal case + payout math | DOJ Michael Smith; Deezer update | Exact payout systems vary. |
| Clipping/trend simulation can create perceived ubiquity | Reporting + mechanism | BI, WIRED, TechCrunch | Causal attribution to sales/streams often hidden. |
